mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A month-over-month decrease in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days as market reprices equity risk premium downward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals robust labor demand and strengthening aggregate demand, which increases corporate profit expectations. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS of more than 100,000 persons triggers positive equity returns within 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer spending strength and near-term economic momentum. When RSAFS accelerates above its 3-month trend by more than 2%, S&P 500 generates positive returns over the subsequent 20 trading days due to upward earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds 3-month moving average by > 2%
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation with forward returns < 0.08 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth indicates capacity utilization and manufacturing strength, signaling robust corporate profitability. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 60 trading days as market reprices cyclical earnings downward.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS growth slows below 50,000 persons monthly, the joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days due to recession risk repricing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 50,000
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower Rank IC than either component alone over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and retail sales jointly capture consumer demand strength. When HOUST and RSAFS both accelerate simultaneously (each > 1-month prior level), S&P 500 generates positive returns within 20 trading days as market reprices consumer-driven earnings growth upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > prior month AND RSAFS > prior month simultaneously
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either component alone over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 3% combined with elevated federal funds rate above 3% signals restrictive monetary policy. Under this regime, S&P 500 forward returns are negative within 60 trading days as real discount rates remain elevated and growth expectations compress.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 57% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect produces lower predictive power than FEDFUNDS alone over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic activity. When GDPC1 is in the top quartile of its historical distribution relative to trend, S&P 500 generates positive returns within 60 trading days as market reprices earnings growth expectations upward in strong growth regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized over prior quarter
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.
